content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data," but RunSessionManager.swift imports CoreLocation, calls requestWhenInUseAuthorization() and startUpdatingLocation(), and stores route history, and the app description explicitly advertises "maps every run with GPS."
Pierre: Apple checks that your privacy policy narrative matches actual app behavior, and a direct contradiction like this is a classic rejection trigger under 5.1.1(i). Here the code clearly collects and retains location data (route: [CLLocation]) while the policy denies any location collection at all. Please update the privacy policy to accurately disclose GPS/location collection, retention, and iCloud sync, matching the App Privacy "Nutrition Label" declarations.
